data.description:

Approach 1: Lamb Weston Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required rate of return.

For Lamb Weston Holdings, Simply Wall St uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $244.9 million. Analyst inputs are available out to 2030, with projected free cash flow of $720.0 million in that year, and the intervening years filled by a mix of analyst estimates and extrapolations provided by Simply Wall St.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of US$146.32 per share. Compared with the most recent share price of US$40.83, this implies the stock is about 72.1% below that DCF estimate. This suggests Lamb Weston is trading at a steep discount to this particular cash flow based valuation.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Lamb Weston Holdings Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings, which makes it a useful cross check against a DCF estimate.

In simple terms, higher expected earnings growth and lower risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower P/E. What feels like a “normal” or “fair” P/E therefore depends on both the company’s outlook and how stable those earnings appear to be.

Lamb Weston Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 14.45x. That sits close to the peer average of 14.46x and below the wider Food industry average P/E of about 20.50x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 18.87x, which is the P/E that might be expected once factors like earnings growth, profit margins, size and risk profile are taken into account.

This Fair Ratio is often more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for company specific traits rather than assuming all firms deserve the same multiple. With the current P/E of 14.45x below the Fair Ratio of 18.87x, the shares appear undervalued on this measure.

data.title: AM Best Rating Affirmation Supports OneMain Insurance Strength And Investor Focus
data.description:
• AM Best affirmed the credit ratings and outlook for OneMain Holdings’ insurance subsidiaries, reinforcing its view of their financial strength.
  • The decision reflects AM Best’s current assessment of the subsidiaries’ balance sheet quality, risk management and operating performance.

For investors watching OneMain Holdings (NYSE:OMF), this rating affirmation comes alongside a mixed share price record. The stock closed at $53.05, with a 4.0% gain over the past week offsetting a 3.9% decline over the past 30 days and a 23.2% decline year to date. Over longer periods, the shares are up 13.3% over 1 year, 88.5% over 3 years and 53.2% over 5 years.

The AM Best decision focuses specifically on the insurance subsidiaries, which are important to the group’s funding and risk profile. For investors, stable credit ratings can be one input when assessing how the business is positioned to support its obligations and operate under different conditions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for OneMain Holdings by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on OneMain Holdings.

Is OneMain Holdings's balance sheet strong enough for future acquisitions? Dive into our detailed financial health analysis.

AM Best’s decision to affirm a B++ Financial Strength Rating and “bbb” Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings for American Health and Life Insurance Company and Triton Insurance Company keeps OneMain’s insurance platform on a stable footing from a regulatory and counterparty perspective. For you as an investor, this signals that an independent assessor currently views these entities as having very strong balance sheet strength, strong operating performance and appropriate risk management. This is important when a group relies on insurance subsidiaries for fee income, ancillary products and funding diversification. A stable outlook also means AM Best is not currently indicating plans for near term upgrades or downgrades, which reduces immediate rating related uncertainty. There is no mention of fines, capital add ons or new operating restrictions tied to this action, so the news is more about confirming the status quo than forcing change. The key watchpoint is that any future deterioration in credit metrics, underwriting results or capital buffers at these subsidiaries could prompt a different stance from AM Best and potentially affect how regulators, lenders and distribution partners view OneMain’s overall risk profile.

How This Fits Into The OneMain Holdings Narrative

  • The affirmation supports the narrative’s focus on disciplined credit management and risk controls by indicating that an external rater views the insurance subsidiaries as having very strong balance sheets and appropriate enterprise risk management.
  • The narrative highlights regulatory threats to the high yield lending model, and this news underlines that regulatory and rating scrutiny remains an ongoing factor that could still pressure returns if standards tighten in the future.
  • The specific role of the insurance subsidiaries in funding flexibility and cross selling is only briefly touched on in the narrative, so this rating action may not be fully reflected in how investors frame the durability of those revenue streams.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for OneMain Holdings to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 2 key risks, including concerns that debt is not well covered by operating cash flow, which can matter if funding markets become less supportive.
  • ⚠️ Regulatory interventions such as interest rate caps or tighter consumer protection rules could weigh on the high yield lending and insurance add on model that supports OneMain’s earnings mix.
  • 🎁 Earnings have grown by 53.8% over the past year. They are also forecast to grow further, which can help support capital levels that rating agencies watch closely.
  • 🎁 The stock is described as trading at good value relative to peers and industry, with pricing below one fair value estimate. This may give some investors room to factor in regulatory or credit related uncertainties.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you will want to watch whether AM Best’s stable outlook holds through future review cycles, especially if credit conditions change or claim trends move against the insurance subsidiaries. Any shift in ratings or outlook could feed into funding costs, counterparties’ willingness to do business and how regulators view the group’s resilience. It is also worth tracking OneMain’s capital management across both the lending and insurance entities, since buybacks and dividends draw on the same capital base that supports ratings. Changes to consumer finance regulation, such as new limits on pricing or ancillary insurance products, would be important context for future rating actions.

data.title: Dividend Investors: Don't Be Too Quick To Buy Danel (Adir Yeoshua) Ltd (TLV:DANE) For Its Upcoming Dividend
data.description:
Some investors rely on dividends for growing their wealth, and if you're one of those dividend sleuths, you might be intrigued to know that Danel (Adir Yeoshua) Ltd (TLV:DANE) is about to go ex-dividend in just 2 days. The ex-dividend date generally occurs two days before the record date, which is the day on which shareholders need to be on the company's books in order to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is important because any transaction on a stock needs to have been settled before the record date in order to be eligible for a dividend. Accordingly, Danel (Adir Yeoshua) investors that purchase the stock on or after the 30th of March will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 20th of April.

The company's next dividend payment will be ₪3.8915 per share, and in the last 12 months, the company paid a total of ₪11.52 per share. Last year's total dividend payments show that Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has a trailing yield of 3.1% on the current share price of ₪454.80. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s dividend is reliable and sustainable. That's why we should always check whether the dividend payments appear sustainable, and if the company is growing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Dividends are typically paid from company earnings. If a company pays more in dividends than it earned in profit, then the dividend could be unsustainable. An unusually high payout ratio of 373% of its profit suggests something is happening other than the usual distribution of profits to shareholders. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. It distributed 39% of its free cash flow as dividends, a comfortable payout level for most companies.

It's disappointing to see that the dividend was not covered by profits, but cash is more important from a dividend sustainability perspective, and Danel (Adir Yeoshua) fortunately did generate enough cash to fund its dividend. If executives were to continue paying more in dividends than the company reported in profits, we'd view this as a warning sign. Very few companies are able to sustainably pay dividends larger than their reported earnings.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

When earnings decline, dividend companies become much harder to analyse and own safely. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. Readers will understand then, why we're concerned to see Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s earnings per share have dropped 25% a year over the past five years. When earnings per share fall, the maximum amount of dividends that can be paid also falls.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has delivered 8.8% dividend growth per year on average over the past 10 years. That's intriguing, but the combination of growing dividends despite declining earnings can typically only be achieved by paying out a larger percentage of profits. Danel (Adir Yeoshua) is already paying out 373% of its profits, and with shrinking earnings we think it's unlikely that this dividend will grow quickly in the future.

Final Takeaway

Is Danel (Adir Yeoshua) an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? It's never great to see earnings per share declining, especially when a company is paying out 373% of its profit as dividends, which we feel is uncomfortably high. Yet cashflow was much stronger, which makes us wonder if there are some large timing issues in Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s cash flows, or perhaps the company has written down some assets aggressively, reducing its income. Overall it doesn't look like the most suitable dividend stock for a long-term buy and hold investor.

With that in mind though, if the poor dividend characteristics of Danel (Adir Yeoshua) don't faze you, it's worth being mindful of the risks involved with this business. For example, Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has 3 warning signs (and 1 which makes us a bit uncomfortable) we think you should know about.

data.title: The Strong Earnings Posted By Utron (TLV:UTRN) Are A Good Indication Of The Strength Of The Business
data.description:
The subdued stock price reaction suggests that Utron Ltd's (TLV:UTRN) strong earnings didn't offer any surprises. Investors are probably missing some underlying factors which are encouraging for the future of the company.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

A Closer Look At Utron's Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. While it's not a problem to have a positive accrual ratio, indicating a certain level of non-cash profits, a high accrual ratio is arguably a bad thing, because it indicates paper profits are not matched by cash flow. To quote a 2014 paper by Lewellen and Resutek, "firms with higher accruals tend to be less profitable in the future".

Utron has an accrual ratio of -0.27 for the year to December 2025. That indicates that its free cash flow quite significantly exceeded its statutory profit. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of ₪10m, well over the ₪3.40m it reported in profit. Utron did see its free cash flow drop year on year, which is less than ideal, like a Simpson's episode without Groundskeeper Willie.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of Utron.

Our Take On Utron's Profit Performance

As we discussed above, Utron's accrual ratio indicates strong conversion of profit to free cash flow, which is a positive for the company. Because of this, we think Utron's underlying earnings potential is as good as, or possibly even better, than the statutory profit makes it seem! And on top of that, its earnings per share have grown at an extremely impressive rate over the last year. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. Keep in mind, when it comes to analysing a stock it's worth noting the risks involved. You'd be interested to know, that we found 1 warning sign for Utron and you'll want to know about it.

data.title: Several Insiders Invested In Capita Flagging Positive News
data.description:
Usually, when one insider buys stock, it might not be a monumental event. But when multiple insiders are buying like they did in the case of Capita plc (LON:CPI), that sends out a positive message to the company's shareholders.

While insider transactions are not the most important thing when it comes to long-term investing, we do think it is perfectly logical to keep tabs on what insiders are doing.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Capita Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

The CFO & Director Pablo Andres made the biggest insider purchase in the last 12 months. That single transaction was for UK£100k worth of shares at a price of UK£2.77 each. That means that even when the share price was higher than UK£2.77 (the recent price), an insider wanted to purchase shares. While their view may have changed since the purchase was made, this does at least suggest they have had confidence in the company's future. To us, it's very important to consider the price insiders pay for shares. It is generally more encouraging if they paid above the current price, as it suggests they saw value, even at higher levels.

Capita insiders may have bought shares in the last year, but they didn't sell any. You can see the insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year depicted in the chart below. If you click on the chart, you can see all the individual transactions, including the share price, individual, and the date!

See our latest analysis for Capita

There are plenty of other companies that have insiders buying up shares. You probably do not want to miss this free list of undervalued small cap companies that insiders are buying.

Capita Insiders Bought Stock Recently

Over the last quarter, Capita insiders have spent a meaningful amount on shares. In total, insiders bought UK£301k worth of shares in that time, and we didn't record any sales whatsoever. This makes one think the business has some good points.

Does Capita Boast High Insider Ownership?

Another way to test the alignment between the leaders of a company and other shareholders is to look at how many shares they own. We usually like to see fairly high levels of insider ownership. Our data suggests Capita insiders own 0.9% of the company, worth about UK£2.7m. I generally like to see higher levels of ownership.

So What Do The Capita Insider Transactions Indicate?

The recent insider purchases are heartening. And an analysis of the transactions over the last year also gives us confidence. However, we note that the company didn't make a profit over the last twelve months, which makes us cautious. While the overall levels of insider ownership are below what we'd like to see, the history of transactions imply that Capita insiders are reasonably well aligned, and optimistic for the future. In addition to knowing about insider transactions going on, it's beneficial to identify the risks facing Capita.
